name: Laguna del Rincón — circular desde Moriles
region_intro: Jogging around Moriles offers a chance to explore the rolling agricultural landscapes of Andalusia, characterized by extensive olive groves and vineyards. The region features a mix of gentle hills and flatter sections, including converted railway lines known as Vías Verdes, which provide accessible running surfaces. These routes often pass by local lagoons and offer views of the surrounding countryside, making for varied running experiences. Best jogging routes around Moriles The most popular jogging route is Laguna del Rincón — circular desde Moriles, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic loop around a local lagoon. Another top favourite among local runners is Vía Verde del Aceite - Moriles Loop Trail, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. This running route follows a converted railway line, winding through olive groves and rural landscapes. Local runners also love the Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil, a 16.6 miles (26.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past historical points, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Jogging around Moriles is defined by its olive groves, vineyards, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths along Vías Verdes to more challenging routes with greater elevation. The routes in Moriles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 133 runners have used komoot to explore Moriles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Moriles?
A: There are over 20 running routes around Moriles, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. You'll find options ranging from easy paths to more challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Moriles?
A: Yes, Moriles offers several routes suitable for beginners. The region's Vías Verdes, converted railway lines, provide accessible and relatively flat surfaces, perfect for an easier run. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286081351/laguna-del-rincon-circular-desde-moriles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rincón — circular desde Moriles&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.1 km loop.

Q: What are the typical lengths of jogging trails in Moriles?
A: Running trails in Moriles vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes around 6-7 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Aguilar de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, up to longer, more challenging runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 26 km.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Moriles?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Moriles are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286081351/laguna-del-rincon-circular-desde-moriles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rincón — circular desde Moriles&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop around a local lagoon.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Moriles?
A: The running trails in Moriles showcase the beautiful agricultural landscapes of Andalusia. You'll typically run through extensive olive groves and vineyards, with views of gentle hills and local lagoons. The Vías Verdes also offer a unique perspective on the rural countryside.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Moriles?
A: The running routes in Moriles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from accessible Vías Verdes to routes with more elevation, and the scenic beauty of the olive groves and vineyards.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting points. You might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt;, or historical elements such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; and old railway stations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Lucena Station&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/893911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla Vineyards&lt;/a&gt; are also a prominent feature of the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Moriles?
A: Moriles, located in Andalusia, experiences hot summers. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with mild weather ideal for exploring the trails. Winter can also be suitable, though mornings might be cooler.

Q: Are there any specific considerations for running in Moriles during summer or winter?
A: During summer, it's crucial to run early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the intense midday heat. Hydration is key. In winter, while generally mild, mornings can be cool, so dressing in layers is advisable. The Vías Verdes offer good all-weather surfaces.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Vía Verde del Aceite near Moriles?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde del Aceite is a significant feature of the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286068080/via-verde-del-aceite-moriles-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite - Moriles Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 13.7 km running route that follows this converted railway line, winding through olive groves.

Q: Can I find less crowded running routes in Moriles?
A: While popular routes like the Vía Verde del Aceite can see some activity, Moriles offers many trails through its extensive agricultural landscapes. Exploring some of the less-trafficked paths through the olive groves and vineyards can provide a more solitary running experience.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by lagoons or water features?
A: Yes, some routes in Moriles offer views of local lagoons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1286081351/laguna-del-rincon-circular-desde-moriles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rincón — circular desde Moriles&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing a scenic loop around a beautiful lagoon.
